message:
chore: rolling sync from concurrent agents

Capturing latest concurrent agent changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -773,7 +773,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5","title":"Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX","description":"Refactor `ftui-demo-showcase` to (1) eliminate per-frame allocations in VisualEffectsScreen, (2) adopt extracted `ftui-extras` FX for metaballs/plasma, and (3) optionally keep hi-res rendering via canvas adapters.

This bead is intentionally phased to unblock perf work early:
- `bd-l8x9.5.1`: Painter/Canvas reuse (no per-frame allocations)
- `bd-l8x9.5.2`: metaballs/plasma via extracted BackdropFx (cell-space)
- `bd-l8x9.5.3` (optional): regain hi-res via canvas adapters
- `bd-l8x9.5.4`: fix CLI help text numbering so humans + scripts agree

## Acceptance Criteria
- VisualEffectsScreen is allocation-free in steady state.
- Metaballs/plasma paths in the demo are driven by `ftui-extras` (no duplicated algorithms).
- No behavior regressions in input handling or transitions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:50.704445082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":535,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Current Problem

`VisualEffectsScreen::view()` currently does:
- `let mut painter = Painter::for_area(...)` each frame

This allocates (pixels + colors vectors) every frame and undermines "insanely high performance".

## Refactor Goals

- Metaballs and Plasma should call the extracted `ftui-extras` code (no duplicated algorithms).
- Avoid per-frame Painter allocation by storing painter(s) inside screen state and resizing only on terminal resize.
- Keep existing transitions/controls.

## Implementation Notes

Approach A (preferred):
- Extract effect *core* so it can render both:
  - cell-space (BackdropFx)
  - painter-space (Canvas adapter)
- Demo uses painter-space to keep the wow factor.

Approach B (acceptable interim):
- Use Backdrop-only rendering for metaballs/plasma in the demo (lower-res but still good), while other effects keep using painter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No per-frame Painter allocations in steady state.
- [ ] Metaballs and Plasma demo visuals are driven by library code.
- [ ] No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:14Z"},{"id":536,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Plan Update: Split Into Phases (Unblock Perf Work)

This issue was previously blocked on `bd-l8x9.3`/`.4` (extracted effects) and `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters).

That made the demo perf fix unnecessarily wait on library extraction.

New structure:
- Phase 1: stop per-frame allocations in `VisualEffectsScreen` (Painter reuse + resize-only growth)
- Phase 2: switch metaballs/plasma to the extracted `ftui-extras` BackdropFx implementation using **cell-space** rendering (lowest coupling)
- Phase 3 (optional): switch metaballs/plasma back to **hi-res** via canvas adapters (keeping the wow factor) once adapters exist

Each phase is tracked as its own subtask with the correct blocking dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","title":"Demo Perf: Reuse Painter Buffers (No Per-Frame Allocations)","description":"## Goal
Refactor VisualEffectsScreen to reuse `Painter`/`Canvas` buffers across frames (grow-only), eliminating steady-state allocations.

## Scope
- Cache painter buffers in screen state.
- Resize-only growth; clear on size changes.
- Keep per-effect state isolated from shared buffers.

## Tests / Validation
- Proxy allocation test: stable terminal size does not grow painter buffer capacity after warm-up.
- Unit test: resize triggers growth and clears appropriately.
- E2E: `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` should exercise the VFX screen once screen 12 is included.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No per-frame buffer growth after warm-up at a stable terminal size.
- No behavior regressions in VFX rendering/transitions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:28.355358061Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:28.355333815Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":537,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Notes

Target file:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Current anti-pattern:
- `Painter::for_area(...)` per frame (allocates `pixels` + `colors` vectors every frame).

Refactor sketch:
- Store one or more `Painter` instances inside `VisualEffectsScreen` state.
- Track the last required subpixel dimensions and `Mode`.
- On render:
  - compute required subpixel dims from `area` and `Mode`
  - if dims unchanged: `painter.clear()` and reuse
  - if dims grew: reallocate once, then reuse
  - if dims shrank: keep capacity; just treat as smaller logical size

Validation:
- Run `./scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (screen navigation exercises the visual effects screen).
- Add a focused no-alloc check (unit test or debug-only counter) that asserts no `Vec` growth occurs after the first render at a stable size.

Acceptance:
- [ ] steady-state frames in VisualEffectsScreen allocate 0 bytes
- [ ] resize triggers at most one reallocation per growth event
- [ ] no behavior regressions (transitions/controls still work)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:50Z"},{"id":538,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented painter buffer reuse in VisualEffectsScreen: cached Painter in state with grow-only sizing via Painter::ensure_for_area, render via CanvasRef (no cloning), and added unit tests for stable-size reuse and grow-only resize. Added Painter::buffer_len/ensure_size helpers and CanvasRef in ftui-extras. fmt/check/clippy pass.","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:16:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","title":"Demo Integration Phase 2: Use Extracted Metaballs/Plasma Backdrops (Cell-Space)","description":"## Goal
Switch VisualEffectsScreen metaballs/plasma to call the extracted `ftui-extras` FX code via the Backdrop widget (cell-space rendering).

## Why Cell-Space First
This phase validates composability + code reuse with the lowest coupling.
Hi-res rendering can return later via canvas adapters (bd-l8x9.6 + bd-l8x9.5.3).

## Scope
- Replace demo-owned metaballs/plasma algorithm code with calls into `ftui-extras`.
- Render backdrop first, then render existing overlays (help/FPS) above.

## Tests / Validation
- Covered by `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` once screen 12 is included (bd-l8x9.8.1).
- Add a focused regression check: starting the VFX screen must not panic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Metaballs/plasma demo output is driven by `ftui-extras` (no local copies).
- No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
- Plays nicely with scrim/legibility defaults when overlays are present.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"GrayCompass","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:47:52.587032950Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:47:52.587013193Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":539,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This phase intentionally prioritizes **code reuse + composability validation** over maximum visual fidelity.

Why:
- It proves the extracted `BackdropFx` + `Backdrop` widget can drive a real screen.
- It eliminates duplicated metaballs/plasma logic inside `visual_effects.rs`.
- It gives us a working baseline while `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters) remains optional.

Implementation outline:
- In `VisualEffectsScreen::view()`:
  - for `EffectType::Metaballs` and `EffectType::Plasma`, render a `Backdrop<MetaballsFx/PlasmaFx>` into the screen area.
  - render any existing help/FPS overlay text after the backdrop so it remains readable.
- Keep the rest of the effects unchanged (they can continue using the existing canvas painter).

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma visual output is driven by `ftui-extras` (no local algorithm copies)
- [ ] screen transitions and keyboard controls unchanged
- [ ] no per-frame allocations (relies on Phase 1)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:13Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","title":"Demo Integration Phase 3 (Optional): Hi-Res Metaballs/Plasma via Canvas Adapters","description":"## Goal (Optional)
Restore the showcase "wow" for metaballs/plasma by rendering them at sub-cell resolution via canvas adapters while still using the same extracted core algorithms.

## Scope
- Use the canvas adapters from `bd-l8x9.6` to render metaballs/plasma into a reused `Painter`.
- Convert to `Canvas` and render.
- Keep the Painter reuse work from `bd-l8x9.5.1`.

## Tests / Validation
- E2E smoke coverage: starting the VFX screen must not panic (bd-l8x9.8.1).
- Adapter-specific tests live under `bd-l8x9.6.*`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hi-res metaballs/plasma match or exceed current demo fidelity.
- No duplicated core algorithms inside the demo.
- No per-frame allocations in steady state.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:49:43.928728120Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":540,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This is the fidelity step: once `bd-l8x9.6` exists, the demo can regain sub-cell resolution effects while still using the library code.

Implementation outline:
- Replace the Phase 2 cell-space backdrop rendering with:
  - `MetaballsFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `PlasmaFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `Canvas::from_painter(&painter)` to render
- Keep Painter reuse from Phase 1.

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma match (or exceed) current wow factor
- [ ] no duplicated core algorithms inside demo
- [ ] no per-frame allocations","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:27Z"}]}

All effects now render at Braille sub-pixel resolution. No duplicated algorithms, no per-frame allocations.","created_at":"2026-02-03T16:56:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","title":"Demo UX: Fix CLI Help Screen List (Include VFX + Correct Numbering)","description":"## Task
Update `ftui-demo-showcase --help` screen list so numbering/order matches `ScreenId::ALL` (including VisualEffects as screen 12).

## Scope
- Sync the help table in `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` with the real screen order in `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`.
- Ensure descriptions and numbering are correct.
- Clarify keybindings:
  - number keys cover only the first 10 screens (`1..9` and `0`)
  - Tab cycles through all screens (including 11/12)

## Tests / Validation
- Unit test: help screen list length matches `ScreenId::ALL.len()`.
- Regression test: help text contains the VisualEffects entry as screen 12.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Help output matches reality (no mismatch between docs and actual screen order).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:37.246272720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:03.333050105Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:58:03.333029556Z","close_reason":"Completed: synced CLI help screen list with ScreenId::ALL, added Responsive Demo as screen 14, added 3 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","docs","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":541,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

`crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` has a hardcoded screen list that currently diverges from `ScreenId::ALL` (see `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`).

This causes:
- user confusion (`--screen=N` help doesn’t match actual screens)
- E2E scripts that appear to contradict help text

## Change

- Update the HELP_TEXT screen table to match the real order + include VisualEffects as screen 12.
- Consider updating the keybinding docs too:
  - number keys cover only 1..10 (`1..9` and `0`), while Tab cycles all.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `--help` screen list matches real screen order
- [ ] VisualEffects is documented as screen 12
- [ ] Keybinding docs reflect the 12-screen reality","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.6","title":"Optional: Canvas Adapters for Extracted FX (Metaballs/Plasma)","description":"## Goal
Provide optional canvas adapters so extracted FX can render via `Painter`/Braille for higher effective resolution (showcase wow), without duplicating core math.

## Scope
- Shared sampler (bd-l8x9.6.1) used by both cell-space and painter-space.
- Two adapters:
  - metaballs -> Painter (bd-l8x9.6.2)
  - plasma -> Painter (bd-l8x9.6.3)

## Feature Gating
- Adapters must not force canvas usage for backdrop users.
- Gate adapter modules behind `cfg(feature = "canvas")` + `visual-fx`.

## Tests / Validation
- Adapter regression tests snapshot the rendered glyph grid for a tiny painter->canvas->buffer pipeline.
- No-alloc proxy tests: stable painter sizes do not grow buffers after warm-up.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Demo can regain hi-res metaballs/plasma via adapters (bd-l8x9.5.3).
- No duplicated sampling math between targets.
- Canvas adapters compile out cleanly when `canvas` is disabled.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:21.416869056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:47:59.843085487Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T16:47:59.843064177Z","close_reason":"Canvas adapters complete: PlasmaCanvasAdapter and MetaballsCanvasAdapter use shared sampling API. Feature-gated behind canvas+visual-fx. All 9 tests pass. Compiles out cleanly when canvas disabled.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":542,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Exists

The showcase demo currently renders metaballs/plasma in sub-cell space via Braille for maximum wow.
If we only extract BackdropFx (cell-space), the demo becomes lower-res.

This task keeps the wow factor while still eliminating duplicated algorithms.

## Strategy

- Keep the effect *core* independent of render target.
- Provide an adapter that can fill a `Painter` by sampling the core at sub-pixel coords.
- Feature-gate behind `canvas` (and potentially `visual-fx`) so CPU-only users don't pay for it.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Demo metaballs/plasma use extracted code, not local copies.
- [ ] No per-frame allocations introduced by the adapter.
- [ ] Adapter code stays small and doesn't infect the BackdropFx API.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.6.1","title":"Canvas Adapter Subtask: Shared Core Sampling API (Target-Agnostic)","description":"## Goal
Define a tiny, target-agnostic sampling surface so metaballs/plasma math has a **single source of truth** and can render to:
- cell-space backdrops (w*h)
- `ftui_extras::canvas::Painter` (sub-cell)
- future GPU compute (conceptually the same sampling function)

## Proposed Shape
- A pure sampling function (or small trait) like:
  - `sample(x_norm, y_norm, t, theme, quality, params) -> PackedRgba`

Where:
- `x_norm`/`y_norm` are stable normalized coordinates (define exact convention: [0,1] at centers).
- `t` is explicit.
- No hidden globals.

## Coordinate Mapping Rules (Must Be Explicit)
- Define whether sampling uses:
  - cell centers (`(x+0.5)/w`)
  - subpixel centers for painter (`(px+0.5)/pw`)
- Define whether aspect ratio correction is applied (and how).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: coordinate mapping is stable at boundaries (0, 1, edges, centers).
- Unit tests: sample output is bounded (RGBA channels in 0..=255) and deterministic.
- Regression test: a small set of fixed sample points (x,y,t) produce stable hashes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Effects use this sampler as the only source of truth (no duplicated math between cell and painter paths).
- No allocations inside sampling.
- Sampling conventions are documented so future targets (GPU) match CPU.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:40:48.778595612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T16:42:43.362458573Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T16:42:43.362440428Z","close_reason":"Implemented shared core sampling API","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":543,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Idea:
- Provide a function like `sample(x_norm, y_norm, t, params, theme) -> PackedRgba`.
- Cell-space renderer loops over cells and calls sample.
- Painter-space adapter loops over sub-pixels and calls sample.

Acceptance:
- [ ] single source of truth for the math
- [ ] no allocations in sampling
- [ ] easy to add future targets (e.g., GPU compute)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:40:54Z"}]}

@@ -14,14 +14,25 @@ source "$LIB_DIR/logging.sh"
 source "$LIB_DIR/pty.sh"
 
 JSONL_FILE="$E2E_RESULTS_DIR/terminal_quirks.jsonl"
-RUN_ID="terminal_quirks_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)_$$"
+RUN_ID="${TERMINAL_QUIRKS_RUN_ID:-terminal_quirks_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)_$$}"
+SEED="${TERMINAL_QUIRKS_SEED:-0}"
+DETERMINISTIC="${TERMINAL_QUIRKS_DETERMINISTIC:-0}"
+RUN_START_MS="$(date +%s%3N)"
+
+if [[ "$DETERMINISTIC" == "1" ]]; then
+    RUN_ID="${TERMINAL_QUIRKS_RUN_ID:-terminal_quirks_deterministic}"
+fi
 
 jsonl_log() {
     local line="$1"
     mkdir -p "$E2E_RESULTS_DIR"
     printf '%s\n' "$line" >> "$JSONL_FILE"
 }
 
+json_escape() {
+    printf '%s' "$1" | sed -e 's/\\/\\\\/g' -e 's/"/\\"/g'
+}
+
 sha256_file() {
     local file="$1"
     if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1 && [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
@@ -37,11 +48,13 @@ if ! CANON_BIN="$(resolve_canonicalize_bin)"; then
     for t in screen_immediate_wrap tmux_nested_cursor windows_no_alt_screen; do
         log_test_skip "$t" "pty_canonicalize binary missing"
         record_result "$t" "skipped" 0 "$LOG_FILE" "binary missing"
-        jsonl_log "{\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"case\":\"$t\",\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"binary missing\"}"
+        jsonl_log "{\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"case\":\"$t\",\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"binary missing\",\"seed\":\"$SEED\",\"deterministic\":$DETERMINISTIC}"
     done
     exit 0
 fi
 
+jsonl_log "{\"run_id\":\"$RUN_ID\",\"ts_ms\":${RUN_START_MS},\"event\":\"start\",\"seed\":\"$SEED\",\"deterministic\":$DETERMINISTIC,\"env\":{\"term\":\"$(json_escape "${TERM:-}")\",\"colorterm\":\"$(json_escape "${COLORTERM:-}")\",\"no_color\":\"$(json_escape "${NO_COLOR:-}")\",\"hostname\":\"$(json_escape "$(hostname 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)")\"},\"capabilities\":{\"profiles\":\"screen,tmux_nested,windows_console\"}}"
+
 run_case() {
     local name="$1"
     local profile="$2"
@@ -60,6 +73,10 @@ run_case() {
     log_test_start "$name"
 
     printf '%b' "$input_bytes" > "$input_file"
